title: Quiz 6
subtitle: B1 Thinking Skills Mix
source: Original B1 quiz created for this system

questions.question: I am really tired because I _____ for my science project all afternoon.
questions.explanation: Present perfect continuous uses have/has been + verb-ing. It shows an activity that started earlier and is still important now.

questions.question: By the time we arrived at the cinema, the film _____.
questions.explanation: Past perfect uses had + past participle. The film started before we arrived.

questions.question: I am not used to _____ so early on weekends.
questions.explanation: Be used to means 'be familiar with'. It is followed by a noun or the -ing form: used to waking.

questions.question: Which sentence is correct?
questions.explanation: In indirect questions, use normal word order: where the library is, not where is the library.
options: Do you know where is the library?
Do you know where the library is?
Do you know where the library?
Do you know where does the library is?

questions.question: _____ you hurry, you will miss the bus.
questions.explanation: Unless means 'if not'. Unless you hurry = if you do not hurry.

questions.question: It was _____ an interesting story that I read it twice.
questions.explanation: Use such + a/an + adjective + noun: such an interesting story. Use so before just an adjective: so interesting.

questions.question: This backpack is _____ heavy for me to carry.
questions.explanation: Too + adjective means 'more than is good or possible': too heavy for me to carry.

questions.question: Sam is smiling and holding a trophy. He _____ the race.
questions.explanation: Must have + past participle shows a strong guess about the past based on evidence.

questions.question: New Zealand is in _____ Pacific Ocean.
questions.explanation: We use the with oceans, seas, rivers and mountain ranges: the Pacific Ocean.

questions.question: This is the town _____ my grandparents met for the first time.
questions.explanation: Use where for places in relative clauses: the town where they met.

questions.question: Please remember _____ the door before you leave.
questions.explanation: Remember to do something means do not forget to do it in the future.

questions.question: The teacher said, 'Be quiet.' → The teacher told us _____.
questions.explanation: Reported commands use told + person + to + verb: told us to be quiet.

questions.question: My bike _____, so I can ride it again.
questions.explanation: Present perfect passive uses has/have been + past participle: has been repaired.

questions.question: _____ feeling nervous, Mia gave a clear presentation.
questions.explanation: Despite is followed by a noun or -ing form: despite feeling nervous.

questions.question: I have _____ friends in my new class, so I do not feel lonely.
questions.explanation: A few means some, enough to be positive. Few means not many and sounds negative.

questions.question: Could you _____ the music? I am trying to read.
questions.explanation: Turn down means make something quieter. Turn up means make it louder.

questions.question: Daniel is _____ good progress in English this month.
questions.explanation: The natural collocation is make progress, not do progress.

questions.question: The team was very _____ because they practised every day. (success)
questions.explanation: Success is a noun. Successful is the adjective: a successful team.

questions.question: She _____ the guitar for three years. (learn)
questions.explanation: Use has been + verb-ing for an activity that started in the past and continues now.

questions.question: I failed the test. I _____ harder. (should / study)
questions.explanation: Should have + past participle is used for advice or regret about the past.

questions.question: Put the words in order to make an indirect question:
questions.correctSentence: Can you tell me where the station is ?
questions.explanation: Indirect questions use normal word order after the question phrase: where the station is.

questions.question: Put the words in the correct order:
questions.correctSentence: I have been learning English for two years
questions.explanation: Present perfect continuous: have been + verb-ing + for/since time expression.

questions.reading: Every Thursday after school, Noah goes to Eco Club. At first, he joined because his best friend was there, but now he really enjoys it. Last month, the club planted trees beside the football field and made posters about saving water. Noah used to forget to turn off the tap while brushing his teeth, but now he reminds his family to save water too. The teacher says small actions can become big changes when many people do them together.
questions.question: Why did Noah first join Eco Club?
questions.explanation: The text says he first joined because his best friend was there.
options: Because he wanted to plant trees
Because his best friend was there
Because his teacher made him join

questions.reading: Every Thursday after school, Noah goes to Eco Club. At first, he joined because his best friend was there, but now he really enjoys it. Last month, the club planted trees beside the football field and made posters about saving water. Noah used to forget to turn off the tap while brushing his teeth, but now he reminds his family to save water too. The teacher says small actions can become big changes when many people do them together.
questions.question: What did the club do last month?
questions.explanation: They planted trees beside the football field and made posters about saving water.
options: They planted trees and made posters

questions.reading: Every Thursday after school, Noah goes to Eco Club. At first, he joined because his best friend was there, but now he really enjoys it. Last month, the club planted trees beside the football field and made posters about saving water. Noah used to forget to turn off the tap while brushing his teeth, but now he reminds his family to save water too. The teacher says small actions can become big changes when many people do them together.
questions.question: What has changed about Noah?
questions.explanation: He has become more careful about saving water and now reminds his family too.
options: He has become more careful about saving water
He wants to leave the club

questions.question: Can I _____ your ruler for a minute? I will give it back soon.
questions.explanation: Borrow means take something from someone for a short time. Lend means give it to someone for a short time.

questions.question: I _____ my cousin at the station at 4 p.m. We arranged it yesterday.
questions.explanation: Use present continuous for fixed future arrangements: I am meeting my cousin.

questions.question: If I _____ more free time, I would join the drama club.
questions.explanation: Second conditional uses if + past simple, would + base verb for imaginary situations.

questions.question: You can play outside _____ you finish your homework first.
questions.explanation: As long as means 'only if'. You can play outside only if you finish your homework first.

questions.question: After a month in New Zealand, I am used to _____ on the left. (cycle)
questions.explanation: After be used to, use a noun or the -ing form: used to cycling.

questions.question: I am looking forward to _____ my friend this weekend.
questions.explanation: Look forward to is followed by a noun or the -ing form: looking forward to seeing.

questions.question: Emma made the birthday card by _____.
questions.explanation: By herself means without help from other people.

questions.question: The film was a _____ story about a young inventor.
questions.explanation: Well-known is a compound adjective meaning famous or known by many people.

questions.question: _____ it was difficult, Daniel finished the puzzle.
questions.explanation: Even though introduces a contrast and is followed by a full clause: even though it was difficult.

questions.question: My teacher gave me _____ about how to improve my writing.
questions.explanation: Advice is uncountable in English. We say some advice or a piece of advice, not an advice.

questions.question: It was a difficult _____, but we chose the best plan. (decide)
questions.explanation: Decide is a verb. Decision is the noun: make a decision.

questions.question: Put the words in the correct order:
questions.correctSentence: If I were you I would ask for help
questions.explanation: A common advice pattern is: If I were you, I would + base verb.

questions.reading: On Friday, Daniel's class visited the city museum. Before the trip, Daniel thought museums were boring, but this one surprised him. In one room, students could touch copies of old tools and guess what people used them for. Daniel's favourite part was a map that lit up to show how the city had grown over 150 years. On the bus home, he told his teacher he wanted to bring his parents there at the weekend.
questions.question: What surprised Daniel?
questions.explanation: He thought museums were boring, but this museum surprised him because it was interesting and interactive.
options: The museum was more interesting than he expected
His parents were at the museum

questions.reading: On Friday, Daniel's class visited the city museum. Before the trip, Daniel thought museums were boring, but this one surprised him. In one room, students could touch copies of old tools and guess what people used them for. Daniel's favourite part was a map that lit up to show how the city had grown over 150 years. On the bus home, he told his teacher he wanted to bring his parents there at the weekend.
questions.question: What was Daniel's favourite part?
questions.explanation: His favourite part was the map that lit up to show how the city had grown.

questions.reading: On Friday, Daniel's class visited the city museum. Before the trip, Daniel thought museums were boring, but this one surprised him. In one room, students could touch copies of old tools and guess what people used them for. Daniel's favourite part was a map that lit up to show how the city had grown over 150 years. On the bus home, he told his teacher he wanted to bring his parents there at the weekend.
questions.question: How did Daniel probably feel after the trip?
questions.explanation: He wanted to bring his parents there, so he probably felt excited and interested.

questions.question: Which sentence is correct?
questions.explanation: Use present perfect with since for something that started in the past and continues now.
options: I have known him since Monday.
I am knowing him since Monday.

questions.question: Nobody called while I was out, _____?
questions.explanation: With negative words like nobody, use a positive question tag. For nobody, the tag usually uses they.

questions.question: I would rather _____ at home tonight. (stay)
questions.explanation: Would rather is followed by the base verb: would rather stay.

questions.question: You _____ leave now, or you will be late.
questions.explanation: Had better + base verb gives strong advice: You had better leave now.

questions.question: I don't like scary films. — I don't like them _____.
questions.explanation: In negative agreement, use either at the end: I don't like them either.

questions.question: I prefer playing chess _____ watching TV.
questions.explanation: With prefer + -ing, use to for comparison: prefer playing chess to watching TV.

questions.question: Please read the instructions _____. (careful)
questions.explanation: Careful is an adjective. Carefully is the adverb used to describe how we do something.

questions.question: The instructions were confusing, so I asked my teacher to _____ them.
questions.explanation: Explain means make something clear or easy to understand.

questions.question: The museum was free, so we _____ pay for tickets.
questions.explanation: Didn't have to means it was not necessary. Mustn't means it was forbidden.

questions.question: Which sentence best shows a plan, not a sudden decision?
questions.explanation: Be going to is often used for plans made before speaking. Will can be used for sudden decisions.
options: I am going to visit my aunt tomorrow.
